vocabulary.definition: International Space Station — a modular space station in low Earth orbit, operated as a joint project between NASA, Roscosmos, ESA, JAXA, and CSA.
An object containing the current latitude and longitude of the point on Earth directly beneath the International Space Station.
The angular distance of a point north or south of the equator, expressed in decimal degrees. Ranges from -90 (South Pole) to 90 (North Pole).
The angular distance of a point east or west of the prime meridian, expressed in decimal degrees. Ranges from -180 to 180.
A Unix timestamp (seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 UTC) indicating the moment when the ISS location data was recorded.
Abbreviation used in the Open Notify API for astronauts — humans currently in space across all crewed spacecraft.
The name of the spacecraft or space station a crew member is currently aboard (e.g., ISS, Shenzhou).
In the astronauts response, the total count of humans currently in space across all spacecraft.
A status string returned in every Open Notify API response, typically 'success' when the request completes without error.
An open-source project by Nathan Bergey that provides simple REST API access to real-time NASA and ISS data without registration or authentication.
An orbit around Earth with an altitude typically between 160 and 2,000 km. The ISS orbits at approximately 400 km altitude.
JSON with Padding — a technique for cross-domain data requests. Open Notify supports JSONP via a callback query parameter.
